steps.title: Identify the constraint on a
steps.content: $\sqrt{6a+7} = -9a$ requires $-9a \geq 0$ (since the square root is non-negative), so $a \leq 0$. This eliminates the positive choices C and D.

steps.title: Backsolve the remaining choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($-\frac{7}{27}$):
If $a = -\frac{7}{27}$, then $-9a = \frac{7}{3}$.
Check: $\sqrt{6(-\frac{7}{27})+7} = \sqrt{-\frac{14}{9} + 7} = \sqrt{\frac{49}{9}} = \frac{7}{3}$ ✓

Both sides equal $\frac{7}{3}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
